name: Nearest Neighbor
summary: Deterministic greedy TSP baseline that grows a tour by repeatedly choosing the nearest unvisited node.
description: Nearest Neighbor is the simplest executable TSP baseline in Rastion. It is intentionally easy to read, emits streaming progress, and provides a cheap reference point for official TSPLIB suite comparisons.

capabilities.notes: Greedy construction only. Useful as a transparent routing baseline.

limits.depot_assumptions: Assumes a single depot and a closed Hamiltonian tour.
limits.notes: Rastion v0.1 supports TSP only. Broader variants are future work.

determinism: Deterministic for a fixed seed; seed only breaks ties between equal-distance candidates.
citations: Nearest-neighbor tour construction is a standard greedy baseline for the travelling salesman problem.
known_failure_modes: Can get trapped in poor local structure on clustered or deceptive TSPLIB instances.
Tour quality degrades quickly as instance size grows.
